name: Elastic-Cam Tossbox
approach: A low-cost, foldable frame houses a replaceable elastic-band powered launch arm driven through selectable cam profiles. Balls are gravity-fed from a small hopper into a single-ball gate; an adjustable mechanical escapement times releases. Variation is achieved by swapping cam inserts and changing arm pre-load and release geometry; simple electronics provide a user-settable mechanical trigger pulse and status LEDs for feedback.
highlights: Purely mechanical energy storage (elastic bands) for very low-cost and lightweight design
Interchangeable cams and preload adjustment produce discrete, repeatable toss patterns
Gravity-fed single-ball gate minimizes double-feeds and simplifies jam clearing
Foldable, tool-free setup and minimal electronics (timer + status LEDs) for portability

name: Dual-Wheel Brushless Pitcher
approach: Two counter-rotating brushless wheels accelerate a ball between them; varying wheel speeds controls toss speed while a yaw/tilt gimbal steers direction. A simple single-ball feed gate and microcontroller coordinate wheel spin-up and feed timing to produce consistent, repeatable tosses or randomized sequences stored in profiles. System runs on a rechargeable battery pack; mesh enclosure and quick-release handles support transport and outdoor use.
highlights: Electromechanical wheel launch gives fine continuous control of speed and horizontal/vertical bias
Microcontroller profiles enable repeatability, randomized training sequences, and live interval adjustment
Rechargeable battery and motor controllers support sustained cycle rates with thermal protection
Robust shrouding and pivoting throw head for aiming and user safety

name: Compressed-Air Soft-Toss Pod
approach: A sealed pressure reservoir charged by a hand pump or small electric pump delivers a short solenoid-valve pulse to a launch chamber, expelling a ball along an adjustable-angled nozzle. Toss speed and height vary by changing reservoir pressure and valve timing; nozzle articulation and a swivel mount adjust lateral aim. Pressure and flow sensors provide closed-loop control to ensure consistent toss energy and detect misfires.
highlights: Pneumatic energy storage separates charging and launch phases for consistent shot energy
Continuous pressure control and sensors enable closed-loop repeatability and variation modes
Simple single-shot feed pocket and purge procedures reduce double-feed risk
Rugged sealed enclosure and hand-pump option for battery-constrained field use
